title: Shell scripting
description: Bash scripts for deployments, backups and log rotation — the kind that check their own exit codes before declaring victory.

title: Package management
description: Comfortable across apt, snap and manual .deb installs — and knowing when to pin a version instead of chasing latest.

title: System administration
description: Systemd units, log inspection with journalctl, and enough monitoring to get paged before a customer notices.

title: Permissions
description: Least-privilege by default: dedicated service users, group-scoped access, and secrets that are never world-readable.

title: Processes
description: Reading process trees, killing what needs killing, and tracing runaway memory back to the code that caused it.

title: Networking commands
description: dig, traceroute, ss and tcpdump for figuring out whether it's DNS, the firewall, or the application (it's usually DNS).

title: SSH
output: Warning: Permanently added '10.0.4.12' (ED25519) to known hosts.
description: Key-based auth only, password login disabled, and a bastion host in front of anything that matters.

title: Cron jobs
description: Nightly backups, health checks every 15 minutes, and weekly log rotation — boring on purpose.

title: Docker
description: Multi-stage builds to keep images small, and Compose stacks for anything that needs more than one container.
